rules.non_negotiable: The sidebar MUST NOT write directly to spec files as a side effect of capture or conflict detection — captures go only to openspec/changes/<session_id>/incremental.json.
The sidebar MUST NOT write directly to spec files as a side effect of capture or conflict detection — captures go only to openspec/changes/<session_id>/incremental.json.
The sidebar MUST NOT make LLM calls synchronously in the main pipe-reading loop; all LLM work MUST run in daemon threads to avoid blocking the event pipe.
The sidebar MUST NOT make LLM calls synchronously in the main pipe-reading loop; all LLM work MUST run in daemon threads to avoid blocking the event pipe.
Hook scripts MUST NOT block; the sidebar is the blocking process and hooks are thin relays that write to the pipe only.
Hook scripts MUST NOT block; the sidebar is the blocking process and hooks are thin relays that write to the pipe only.
Skill scripts MUST use ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}-relative paths for cross-skill references, never hardcoded absolute paths.
Skill scripts MUST use ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}-relative paths for cross-skill references, never hardcoded absolute paths.
architecture.md must never be allowed to drift from the actual implementation — it is the source of truth and a diverged architecture doc is a CRITICAL integrity failure.
architecture.md must never be allowed to drift from the actual implementation — it is the source of truth and a diverged architecture doc is a CRITICAL integrity failure.
The hook architecture non-negotiable documented in architecture.md (hooks are thin relays only, no API calls, no direct spec writes) must never be contradicted or softened in any revision of the doc.
The hook architecture non-negotiable documented in architecture.md (hooks are thin relays only, no API calls, no direct spec writes) must never be contradicted or softened in any revision of the doc.
The append-only invariant for spec lineage and lessons.json must be explicitly stated in architecture.md and must never be removed from it.
The append-only invariant for spec lineage and lessons.json must be explicitly stated in architecture.md and must never be removed from it.
phase-prompts.md must never be edited to retroactively change a story's acceptance criterion after that story has been completed — doing so breaks the audit trail of what was actually built.
phase-prompts.md must never be edited to retroactively change a story's acceptance criterion after that story has been completed — doing so breaks the audit trail of what was actually built.
Checkpoints in checkpoints.md must never be removed once created; they are permanent phase-boundary records.
Checkpoints in checkpoints.md must never be removed once created; they are permanent phase-boundary records.
The protected files list in architecture.md must stay in sync with the protected files list enforced in CLAUDE.md — a discrepancy between the two is a CRITICAL configuration error.
The protected files list in architecture.md must stay in sync with the protected files list enforced in CLAUDE.md — a discrepancy between the two is a CRITICAL configuration error.
The layer rules table in architecture.md (hooks may not import from skills; skills may not call hooks) must never be relaxed without an explicit recorded architectural decision.
The layer rules table in architecture.md (hooks may not import from skills; skills may not call hooks) must never be relaxed without an explicit recorded architectural decision.
Hooks must never make API calls of any kind.
Hooks must never make API calls of any kind.
Hooks must never spawn subprocesses or perform any operation that could block for more than a few milliseconds.
Hooks must never spawn subprocesses or perform any operation that could block for more than a few milliseconds.
Hooks must never write directly to spec files, openspec files, or any file under the .companion/ directory — with the sole exception that session_end writes the closed-session marker to state.json.
Hooks must never write directly to spec files, openspec files, or any file under the .companion/ directory — with the sole exception that session_end writes the closed-session marker to state.json.
Hooks must never perform extraction, conflict detection, UML generation, or any other analytical logic; those responsibilities belong exclusively to the sidebar.
Hooks must never perform extraction, conflict detection, UML generation, or any other analytical logic; those responsibilities belong exclusively to the sidebar.
Hooks must never suppress or auto-approve a Claude Code permission prompt (e.g. ExitPlanMode); the decision must always be passed back to the normal Claude Code approval UI.
Hooks must never suppress or auto-approve a Claude Code permission prompt (e.g. ExitPlanMode); the decision must always be passed back to the normal Claude Code approval UI.
The hooks.json registration file must not be modified without an explicit architectural reason, as it is a protected file governing which lifecycle events anchor intercepts.
The hooks.json registration file must not be modified without an explicit architectural reason, as it is a protected file governing which lifecycle events anchor intercepts.
Lessons are append-only. Existing lesson entries must never be deleted or have any field other than status modified.
Lessons are append-only. Existing lesson entries must never be deleted or have any field other than status modified.
lessons.json must never be written by hooks. Only skill scripts (lesson.py, lesson_review.py) may write to it.
lessons.json must never be written by hooks. Only skill scripts (lesson.py, lesson_review.py) may write to it.
lessons.json must never be written by sidebar.py or any companion-layer code.
lessons.json must never be written by sidebar.py or any companion-layer code.
hooks must never be modified by pairmode-skill scripts; hooks are thin relays and are a protected subsystem
hooks must never be modified by pairmode-skill scripts; hooks are thin relays and are a protected subsystem
hooks must never be modified by pairmode-skill scripts; hooks are thin relays and are a protected subsystem
hooks must never be modified by pairmode-skill scripts; hooks are thin relays and are a protected subsystem
pairmode bootstrap must never overwrite existing project files without explicit per-file user confirmation
pairmode bootstrap must never overwrite existing project files without explicit per-file user confirmation
pairmode bootstrap must never overwrite existing project files without explicit per-file user confirmation
pairmode bootstrap must never overwrite existing project files without explicit per-file user confirmation
spec files (openspec/specs/*/spec.json) must never be written by bootstrap, checklist_deriver, or denylist_deriver; those modules are read-only consumers of spec data
spec files (openspec/specs/*/spec.json) must never be written by bootstrap, checklist_deriver, or denylist_deriver; those modules are read-only consumers of spec data
spec files (openspec/specs/*/spec.json) must never be written by bootstrap, checklist_deriver, or denylist_deriver; those modules are read-only consumers of spec data
spec files (openspec/specs/*/spec.json) must never be written by bootstrap, checklist_deriver, or denylist_deriver; those modules are read-only consumers of spec data
Neither file may be modified without a stated reason — they are declared protected files in the architecture and any change is a protected-file event requiring explicit justification.
Neither file may be modified without a stated reason — they are declared protected files in the architecture and any change is a protected-file event requiring explicit justification.
These files must never be generated, overwritten, or written to by hooks, sidebar.py, or any runtime skill script — they are static declarations, not runtime artifacts.
These files must never be generated, overwritten, or written to by hooks, sidebar.py, or any runtime skill script — they are static declarations, not runtime artifacts.
Lineage entries in spec.json are append-only and must never be rewritten or deleted after creation.
Lineage entries in spec.json are append-only and must never be rewritten or deleted after creation.
Session mining must not run through Task subagents; it must block in the main agent to guarantee all writes complete before reconciliation begins.
Session mining must not run through Task subagents; it must block in the main agent to guarantee all writes complete before reconciliation begins.
The reconciler must never overwrite spec content with conflicting information without flagging the contradiction; contradictions go to conflicts_pending.json.
The reconciler must never overwrite spec content with conflicting information without flagging the contradiction; contradictions go to conflicts_pending.json.
The skill must not write to any hook scripts or protected files; it writes only to .companion/, <spec_location>/openspec/, and product config files under ~/.companion/.
